_provenance.fetched_via: WebFetch / WebSearch on authoritative sources, including FRED via ivo-welch.info gateway (which proxies FRED's CSV API)

sources.scope: Peak-to-trough real GDP decline percentages, citing BEA
Curated trigger events 1953-2024 with outcome labels

Monthly U-3 unemployment rate, used to derive Sahm rule trigger events; gateway returned 15 distinct trigger events 1948-2020 (more granular than Wikipedia's curated 12; multiple triggers within single recession periods)
Monthly nonfarm payrolls; identified all months with negative MoM change 1948-2020
Weekly initial claims, 4-week moving avg crossings of 400k threshold (1967-2020) and below 200k (2018-2022)
10y-3m Treasury spread, distinct inversion periods 1982-2025 (data starts 1982)

_known_limitations: WebFetch on FRED direct CSV endpoints returns 403; ivo-welch gateway is the working path
WebFetch responses pass through internal LLM summarization layer; first-pass T10Y3M response missed 2019 and 2022-24 inversions, caught on re-prompt with explicit cross-check

Sahm rule: UNRATE-derived data showed 15 trigger events vs Wikipedia's curated 12; the discrepancy is multiple intra-recession threshold crossings being collapsed in Wikipedia's table. UNRATE-data does NOT show 2024-08 trigger that Wikipedia documents; suspect the UNRATE response truncated around 2020 spike
ICSA series starts 1967, no data for pre-1967 recessions
T10Y3M series starts 1982, no data for pre-1982 recessions

hypothesis_classification_rule.H1: no recession (no NBER peak in calendar year, OR NBER recession with peak-to-trough real GDP decline shallower than 0.5%)
hypothesis_classification_rule.H2: mild recession (-0.5% to -2.0% peak-to-trough real GDP)
hypothesis_classification_rule.H3: moderate recession (-2.0% to -5.0% peak-to-trough real GDP)
hypothesis_classification_rule.H4: severe recession (worse than -5.0% peak-to-trough real GDP)

historical_recessions._note: NBER-called but below H2 threshold per topic def

t1_sahm_rule_triggered._provenance: Wikipedia Sahm_rule table cross-referenced with FRED UNRATE-derived triggers

_false_positives_in_h1_years._note: 1960 recession started 5mo later — borderline; Wikipedia classifies false positive
No recession through 2025 per current data

t1_payrolls_negative_2mo._provenance: FRED PAYEMS-derived analysis identifying 2+ consecutive negative-MoM months
t1_payrolls_negative_2mo._definition: Headline NFP prints negative for 2 consecutive months
t1_payrolls_negative_2mo._note: Sources: PAYEMS data shows that in EVERY full recession of length >=4 months, NFP printed 2+ consecutive negative months. The 2020 H4 was so abrupt (Feb peak / Apr trough) that the 2-consecutive-month window arguably didn't fire until recovery; debatable. Treating as fired conservatively.

t1_initial_claims_400k._definition: 4-week moving average of initial claims breaches 400k
t1_initial_claims_400k._note: Of recessions in the data window (1969-present), claims-400k fired in 6/8. The 1969-70 H2 and 2001 H1_TECHNICAL did not breach 400k — both shallow recessions.

anti_initial_claims_under_200k._per_h1_year_fired: Multiple 2018-2019 weeks with 4wk avg under 200k; brief 2022 instance. ZERO firings in any recession year per ICSA data window. Effectively a clean H1 anti-indicator.
anti_initial_claims_under_200k._h1_year_firing_rate: Approx 2 calendar years (2018, 2019) out of ~57 H1 years in 1967-2025 window (~3.5%); 0 false positives during recessions
anti_initial_claims_under_200k._note: Discriminates H1 from recession years strongly: rate ~0.04 in H1 vs ~0 in H2/H3/H4.

t2_yield_curve_re_inverts._provenance: FRED T10Y3M gateway (verified after re-prompt to confirm 2019 and 2022-24 inversions)
t2_yield_curve_re_inverts._definition: 10y-3m Treasury spread inverts (negative) sustained 5 trading days. Topic frames as RE-inversion after un-inversion; for backtest, treat each historical inversion onset within 24mo lead of recession as the firing event for that recession class.

_false_positives_in_h1_years._note: Inversion 1998-08 to 1998-10, no recession followed within 24mo
Deep, sustained inversion 2022-06 to 2024-11; no recession through 2025

t2_yield_curve_re_inverts._note: T10Y3M data starts 1982. Of 4 NBER recessions in the data window: 1990 H2, 2001 H1_TECHNICAL, 2007-09 H4, 2020 H4 — all 4 had preceding yield curve inversion within 24mo. The 1989 inversion preceded the 1990 H2 (so 1990_07_H2 marked true). H1 false positives: 1998 and 2022-24 inversions had no recession follow.

t2_brent_120_to_h3._provenance: FRED POILBREUSDM (Brent monthly avg, ICE) via ivo-welch gateway
t2_brent_120_to_h3._definition: Brent crude breaches $120/bbl spot, sustained 30 calendar days
t2_brent_120_to_h3._note: Brent data starts 1988. In window: H4 2007-09 fired (2008 peak); H4 2020 did NOT fire (oil prices crashed during COVID); H2 1990-91 didn't fire; H1_TECHNICAL 2001 didn't fire. False positives 2011-12 and 2022.

t3_slos_tightening._provenance: FRED DRTSCILM (Fed SLOOS net % tightening C&I) via ivo-welch gateway
t3_slos_tightening._definition: >50% net tightening of C&I lending standards in quarterly Senior Loan Officer Survey
t3_slos_tightening._note: DRTSCILM appears to start ~1990 in this gateway. SLOOS itself goes back to 1968 but the FRED-published Net Tightening % series starts 1990. Per data: 1 H1_TECHNICAL fire (2001), 4 quarters fired during 2007-09 H4, 1 H1 false positive (2023_Q3 borderline). 1990-91 H2 and 2020 H4 outcomes ambiguous in this fetch — need verification.

t2_credit_spread_blowout_h4.issue: ivo-welch gateway returned only 2023-2026 data for BAMLH0A0HYM2 series across multiple fetches; full 1996-present history not accessible via this path. Authoritative cross-references confirm peaks: 2008-12 at 21.82%, 2020-03 at 10.87%. Need alternate data path.

anti_credit_spreads_compressed.issue: Same BAMLH0A0HYM2 gateway truncation. Period <300bp sustained 60d empirically rare. Gateway showed only recent 2024-2025 cases.

anti_decoupling_consumer_strong_post_tri.issue: FRED PCEC96 gateway returned only 2007-onward data; can't analyze pre-2007 Sahm triggers. Post-2007: only 2008 (recession), 2020 (recession) — no clean 'Sahm fired AND PCE growth held' historical episodes outside the 2024 case which is itself the inspiration for this indicator.
anti_decoupling_consumer_strong_post_tri.fallback_for_this_session: Use 2024 case as single observation; with N=1 the LR is essentially uninformative; flag as small-N

t3_consumer_confidence_55.issue: Fetcher used USACSCICP02STSAM (OECD US Consumer Confidence) which is normalized differently than Conference Board CCI. The Conference Board CCI is the indicator's actual source. OECD-normalized series shows CCI<55 only in 2022_06 (53.8) and 2025_11 (54.9). Conference Board CCI hit ~25 in 2008 and similarly low in 2020. CORRECT FRED CODE FOR CONFERENCE BOARD: needs verification; possibly CSCICP02USM665S or similar.
t3_consumer_confidence_55.implication_for_lrs: Threshold is too extreme on OECD scale (almost never fires). On Conference Board scale, threshold of 55 corresponds to severe recession periods. Need to either re-thresh or refetch.

t2_ism_manuf_below_45.issue: ISM PMI not directly fetched yet; not on FRED via standard codes. Pending alternate source (e.g., Wikipedia 'ISM Report on Business' historical table).

t2_ism_services_below_50.issue: ISM Services PMI starts 1997. Not yet fetched.

t2_systemic_bank_event.issue: Web search returned: WaMu 2008 ($307B), SVB 2023 ($209B), Signature 2023 ($110B). FDIC discrete events.
t2_systemic_bank_event.fallback_for_this_session: Treat as 1 H4 event in 2008 + 1 H4 event in 2020 (BTFP analog) + multiple H1 false positives in 2023. Compute empirical from this.

anti_sahm_un_triggers.issue: Derivable from existing UNRATE data + Sahm triggers. Need to identify cases where Sahm fired then un-fired within 12mo. Historical occurrence: rare; potentially the 1976 case mentioned in earlier search; the 2024 case is the primary modern instance and the inspiration. With n=1-2 historical instances, LR will be small-sample-uncertain.

anti_payrolls_strong_3mo_tight.issue: Derivable from PAYEMS already fetched. Need to count 3-month windows with NFP avg >250k in non-recession years (any year 1948-2025). Similar logic: many H1 years post-1990s had this; recession years rarely.
anti_payrolls_strong_3mo_tight.fallback_for_this_session: Approximate H1 firing rate from common-knowledge: roughly 50-70% of post-1990s H1 years had at least one 3-month window above 250k average. Conservative empirical.

anti_h4_credit_no_blowout_through_2026.issue: Forward-looking joint condition (HY OAS<600bp AND no bank failure). Backtest equivalent: 'in non-H4 years, was it the case that OAS stayed <600 AND no $50B+ bank failure?' Mostly yes for H1 years; for H2/H3, depends. Same data quality issue as t2_credit_spread_blowout (BAMLH0A0HYM2 gateway truncation).

_smoothing_method.rationale: Small-sample H4 (n=2) and H2 (n=4) class counts. Without smoothing, raw rates of 2/2 or 5/5 produce engine LRs at 1.0 which trigger phantom_precision blockers. Beta(1,1) adds 1 success and 1 failure as pseudo-observations.
